topic.what: Investigation of Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang (Pinellia ternata, Scutellaria baicalensis, Coptis chinensis as core herbs) against antibiotic-resistant H. pylori strains in an animal model of gastritis.
topic.how: In vivo animal experiment: H. pylori-infected rodent model treated with Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang decoction; endpoints include bacterial load, gastric histopathology, inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α), and mucosal integrity markers compared to vehicle and standard antibiotic controls.
topic.why_matters: Clarithromycin and metronidazole resistance is rising globally, threatening first-line eradication; demonstrating that a classical multi-herb formula retains activity against resistant strains could justify clinical trials and offers a resistance-agnostic therapeutic route.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in gastric H. pylori colonisation in antibiotic-resistant infection model
characteristics.value: Significant reduction in bacterial load vs. untreated control; comparable or superior to some antibiotic regimens in resistant strain groups
characteristics.evidence: In vivo animal study — gastric tissue culture and histological scoring
characteristics.context: Animal model results; translational validity to human drug-resistant infection requires clinical validation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in pro-inflammatory cytokine levels in gastric mucosa
characteristics.value: Decreased IL-6 and TNF-α expression in treated animals relative to infected controls
characteristics.context: Cytokine suppression may contribute to mucosal healing independent of direct bactericidal effect

good_for: Researchers exploring non-antibiotic adjuncts for drug-resistant H. pylori
Mechanistic follow-up studies on berberine (from Coptis) and baicalein (from Scutellaria) synergy
KB articles comparing TCM formulas as antibiotic alternatives
not_for: Direct clinical application without human trial data
Strain-specific resistance profiling beyond the tested resistant phenotypes
requires: Animal model expertise for result interpretation
Knowledge of Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang constituent pharmacology

novelty: Specifically tests formula against antibiotic-resistant H. pylori rather than susceptible reference strains, increasing translational relevance
Demonstrates dual bactericidal and anti-inflammatory activity in the same in vivo system
open_problems: Minimum inhibitory concentration data for individual resistant strains not fully characterised
Pharmacokinetic profile of active constituents (berberine, baicalein, wogonin) in gastric tissue not reported
Human RCT in clarithromycin-resistant H. pylori populations absent
